title: ENAUTIC WaveFlyer Rogue-X revealed — 2-seat foiling PWC
summary: ENAUTIC (Australia) revealed two new WaveFlyer Rogue models in March 2024. The Rogue and Rogue-X use the same hull/foil package as the Volaré but with different ergonomics. Max speed 20 knots. Targets family fun and thrill-seekers respectively. Based on patented WaveDrive technology.

title: Valo Hyperfoil prototype launches — speed claims revised down
summary: Boundary Layer Technologies launched its Valo Hyperfoil prototype in June 2025. Top speed revised from claimed 50 knots to 36.5 knots (42 mph) — roughly matching a base-level jet ski. ~60 orders at $59K. Shipping 2025. Company has $7.1M raised and $124M in ferry LOIs.

title: Navier N30 Pioneer II edition launched — targeting superyacht tender market
summary: Navier launched the N30 Pioneer II edition in late 2025. The 30-ft hydrofoiler can carry 9 passengers, reaches 30+ knots, and has 70 nmi range. Starting at ~$850K for cabin model. Being marketed as superyacht tender. Twin 90kW motors with active foil control.

title: Foil.One Pegasus unveiled — $151K Finnish single-seat hydrofoiler
summary: Finnish startup Foil.One revealed the Pegasus in October 2024. Single-seat electric hydrofoil with 18.8kW motor and swappable Torqeedo 5kWh battery packs. Priced at $151,355. Striking minimalist design. Coverage in Electrek.

title: Propel Foil launches 5-in-1 modular watercraft with seated Jet Kart mode
summary: Startup Propel Foil launched crowdfunding in May 2025 for a modular 5-in-1 electric watercraft. Includes eFoil, e-SUP, e-Scooter, and the Jet Kart seated mode. $4,797 pledge. Founded by Jason Toth (ex-pro snowboarder). IP68 waterproofing, jet drive, 3hr battery life.

title: Candela C-8 Polestar edition in production — C-Pod drivetrain key IP
summary: Candela's C-8 continues in production with the Polestar collaboration edition at $450K+. The C-Pod motor (50kW, torpedo-shaped) is the key IP — enabling other boat makers to add hydrofoil propulsion. 8 passengers, 57 nmi range, 22kt cruise speed. Watch their C-Pod licensing strategy.

title: Quadrofoil Q2S: €3M investment (2021) — status uncertain 2024-25
summary: Slovenia's Quadrofoil received €3M in 2021 to scale production of the Q2S two-seater electric hydrofoil. Top speed 21 knots, range ~100km, motorcycle-style 2-up seating. Priced ~€20K originally. Current production status unclear — limited online activity in 2024-25.

title: WIPO patent WO2025089965A1 filed — Norwegian hydrofoil vessel design
summary: New WIPO patent WO2025089965A1 filed Oct 2024 (PCT/NO2024/050216) for a hydrofoil vessel from Norway. Still pending. Worth monitoring for any claims relevant to small electric foiling craft.

title: Market gap confirmed: no direct 'flying couch' product exists yet
summary: Extensive research confirms NO product directly matches the Kai Concepts flying couch concept: a comfortable, sofa/bench-style 2-person electric foiling platform. WaveFlyer Volaré is closest (2-seat side-by-side), but uses jet-ski ergonomics. The true luxury relaxed-seating electric foiler at accessible price points remains unclaimed.
